senses.definition: A plant or shrub of the genus Prunus, bearing sloes, typically a small bluish-black fruit used in making sloe gin and preserves.
examples: The hedgerows were dotted with sloebushes bearing clusters of fruit.
They harvested the sloebush to make traditional plum brandy.

senses.definition: Plural form of sloebush; multiple sloebush plants bearing sloes.
examples: The hedgerows contained several sloebushes loaded with fruit.
Sloebushes along the lane yielded a good harvest this autumn.

senses.definition: The small, dark purple fruits of the blackthorn tree, used to make jams, jellies, and sloe gin.
examples: Sloes are picked in autumn when they are ripe.
She used sloes to brew a tart gin.
The recipe called for a handful of sloes.

senses.definition: An alternate or archaic term for a blackthorn tree, which bears sloes.
examples: The hedge was made from sloethorn branches.
Old recipes mention sloethorn hedges for fruit.

senses.definition: Plural of sloethorn; multiple blackthorn trees.
examples: The bank was lined with sloethorns.
Sloethorns supplied abundant sloes for the harvest.

senses.definition: A tree that bears sloes; another name for a blackthorn tree in some dialects.
examples: They planted a sloetree in the orchard.
The sloetree produced a good crop of fruit that year.

senses.definition: Plural of sloetree; multiple sloetrees.
examples: The path was bordered by sloetrees.
Sloetrees provided good quantities of sloes.

senses.definition: A long, tiring walk or movement; hard, tedious work.
examples: I had a long slog up the hill.
The project was a slog, but we finished it.

senses.definition: To work hard and persistently; to move slowly and with effort.
examples: They slogged through the assignments all week.
She slogged up the staircase with a heavy load.

senses.definition: A short, memorable phrase used in advertising or politics to represent a goal or idea.
examples: The campaign used a catchy slogan.
Their brand slogan emphasizes quality.

senses.definition: Past tense of slog; to have worked hard or to have produced a slogan.
examples: The team sloganed for hours to finish the design.
The campaign sloganed through several revisions.

senses.definition: A person who creates or promotes slogans; a copywriter focusing on slogans.
examples: The sloganeer penned a memorable line for the campaign.
As a sloganeer, she specialized in short, punchy phrases.

senses.definition: Past tense of sloganeer; to have created or promoted slogans.
examples: The agency sloganeered several slogans for the campaign.
He sloganeered tirelessly for the product launch.

senses.definition: The act or practice of creating slogans; the craft of slogan writing.
examples: Sloganeering is common in political campaigns.
The agency specializes in witty sloganeering.

senses.definition: Plural of sloganeering; instances or cases of slogan writing.
examples: The campaign included several sloganeerings across media.
They analyzed different sloganeerings to gauge impact.

senses.definition: People who create or advocate slogans; proponents of slogans.
examples: The sloganeers promoted a strong, memorable line.
Sloganeers often work in marketing teams.

senses.definition: To produce or turn something into a slogan.
examples: They sloganised the new policy to attract voters.
The campaign sloganised the brand essence.

senses.definition: Past tense of sloganise; produced slogans.
examples: The team sloganised the campaign slogans.
They sloganised the message for the event.

senses.definition: Third-person singular present of sloganise; turns something into a slogan.
examples: The team sloganises key ideas for announcements.
Their ads sloganise values like freedom and quality.

senses.definition: The act of creating slogans; the process of turning phrases into slogans.
examples: Sloganising is a key skill in marketing.
They spent hours sloganising different campaign lines.

senses.definition: Plural of sloganising; multiple instances of slogan creation activity.
examples: The agency showcased various sloganisings for different products.
Their sloganisings ranged from serious to playful.

senses.definition: To form or convert into a slogan; to create a catchphrase.
examples: They sloganize the product idea to attract customers.
The campaign sloganizes the message clearly.

senses.definition: Past tense of sloganize; created a slogan from something.
examples: The concept was sloganized into a memorable line.
They sloganized the campaign theme last week.

senses.definition: Third-person singular present of sloganize; creates slogans.
examples: The team sloganizes product ideas for ads.
She sloganizes the core value in every message.

senses.definition: The act of creating slogans; the process of making punchy phrases.
examples: Sloganizing is essential for memorable campaigns.
They spent weeks on sloganizing different ideas.

senses.definition: Plural of sloganizing; multiple slogan-creating activities.
examples: Their sloganisings covered several product lines.
Different departments contributed to the sloganisings.

senses.definition: To create or turn something into a slogan; to phrase as a slogan.
examples: They sloganize the message to appeal to voters.
The brand was sloganized around quality and trust.

senses.definition: Past tense of sloganize; created a slogan from something.
examples: They sloganized the slogan from the campaign concept.
The idea was sloganized and used in posters.

senses.definition: Third-person singular present of sloganize; turns into a slogan.
examples: He sloganizes the product concept for ads.
They sloganizes the core idea in every release.

senses.definition: Plural of sloganizing; multiple slogan-creating activities.
examples: Their sloganizings produced several catchy lines.
Different departments contributed to the sloganizings.
